title: Bold title
text:
The Lost Puppy

Maya heard a tiny whimper coming from behind the bins. She pulled back a cardboard box and saw a small brown puppy shivering in the cold. Around its neck was a red collar with a silver tag.
question: Why is “The Lost Puppy” written in bold?
weakExplanation: That is true, but it does not fully explain why it is bold.
exceptionalAnswer: “The Lost Puppy” is written in bold because it is the title of the story. This makes it stand out so the reader notices it first and understands what the story will be about.

breakdown.label: Reason
breakdown.text: because it is the title of the story
breakdown.note: This answers the basic why.

breakdown.label: Effect
breakdown.text: This makes it stand out
breakdown.note: This explains what the bold writing does.

breakdown.label: Link back to the reader/text
breakdown.text: so the reader notices it first and understands what the story will be about
breakdown.note: This explains why that matters.

title: Character action
text: Tom tiptoed across the hallway, holding his shoes in one hand. He did not switch on the light. His baby sister was asleep in the room next door, and even the smallest noise could wake her.
question: Why did Tom tiptoe across the hallway?
weakAnswer: Tom was walking in the hallway.
weakExplanation: That tells us what he was doing, but not why.
exceptionalAnswer: Tom tiptoed across the hallway because he did not want to wake his baby sister. The text says she was asleep nearby and even a small noise could wake her, so Tom was trying to be quiet.

breakdown.label: Reason
breakdown.text: because he did not want to wake his baby sister
breakdown.note: This directly answers why he tiptoed.

breakdown.label: Evidence from the text
breakdown.text: The text says she was asleep nearby and even a small noise could wake her
breakdown.note: This proves the answer.

breakdown.label: Link back to the action
breakdown.text: so Tom was trying to be quiet
breakdown.note: This connects the reason to the action.

title: Exclamation mark
text: “Watch out!” shouted Aisha, grabbing Leo’s sleeve. A football flew past his face and bounced off the wall behind him.
question: Why does Aisha shout, “Watch out!”?
weakExplanation: That repeats the question but does not explain the reason.
exceptionalAnswer: Aisha shouts “Watch out!” because a football is flying towards Leo and she wants to warn him. She grabs his sleeve to stop him getting hurt.

breakdown.label: Reason
breakdown.text: because a football is flying towards Leo
breakdown.note: This gives the danger.

breakdown.label: Purpose
breakdown.text: and she wants to warn him
breakdown.note: This explains why she shouts.

breakdown.label: Evidence/action
breakdown.text: She grabs his sleeve
breakdown.note: This shows she is trying to protect him.

title: Description of weather
text: Dark clouds rolled over the playground. The wind pushed crisp packets across the concrete, and the first drops of rain splashed onto the climbing frame. Everyone rushed towards the classroom door.
question: Why did everyone rush towards the classroom door?
weakExplanation: That says what happened, not why.
exceptionalAnswer: Everyone rushed towards the classroom door because the weather was getting bad and it had started to rain. They wanted to get inside before they became wet.

breakdown.label: Reason
breakdown.text: because the weather was getting bad and it had started to rain
breakdown.note: This explains why they moved.

breakdown.label: Effect
breakdown.text: They wanted to get inside
breakdown.note: This shows what the rain made them do.

breakdown.label: Link back
breakdown.text: before they became wet
breakdown.note: This explains the purpose of rushing.

title: A character feeling nervous
text: Ella stood outside the music room, holding her violin tightly. Her hands felt sweaty, and her stomach twisted like a knot. Inside, she could hear the judges whispering.
question: Why is Ella holding her violin tightly?
weakExplanation: That is true, but it does not explain why she is holding it tightly.
exceptionalAnswer: Ella is holding her violin tightly because she is nervous about performing. The text says her hands are sweaty and her stomach feels like a knot, which shows she is worried or anxious.

breakdown.label: Reason
breakdown.text: because she is nervous about performing
breakdown.note: This answers the why.

breakdown.label: Evidence
breakdown.text: her hands are sweaty and her stomach feels like a knot
breakdown.note: This proves the feeling.

breakdown.label: Explanation
breakdown.text: which shows she is worried or anxious
breakdown.note: This explains what the clues mean.

title: Repeated word
text:
Run. Run. Run.

Ben could hear the footsteps behind him getting closer. He did not look back. He just kept moving, faster and faster, until he reached the gate.
question: Why has the writer repeated the word “Run”?
weakExplanation: That notices the repetition, but does not explain why it is used.
exceptionalAnswer: The writer repeats the word “Run” to make the moment feel urgent and frightening. The short repeated word makes it sound as if Ben is panicking and needs to escape quickly.

breakdown.label: Reason/purpose
breakdown.text: to make the moment feel urgent and frightening
breakdown.note: This explains why the writer uses repetition.

breakdown.label: Effect
breakdown.text: makes it sound as if Ben is panicking
breakdown.note: This explains what the repetition suggests.

breakdown.label: Link back to the story
breakdown.text: and needs to escape quickly
breakdown.note: This connects the technique to what is happening.

title: A sign
text:
A large red sign stood beside the lake. It said:
DANGER: THIN ICE
Several children stopped at the edge and stepped backwards.
question: Why is the sign large and red?
weakExplanation: That describes the sign, but does not explain the reason for the colour or size.
exceptionalAnswer: The sign is large and red because it needs to warn people about danger quickly. The bright colour and big letters make it easy to notice, so people do not walk onto the thin ice.

breakdown.label: Reason
breakdown.text: because it needs to warn people about danger quickly
breakdown.note: This explains why the sign looks that way.

breakdown.label: Effect
breakdown.text: make it easy to notice
breakdown.note: This explains what large and red do.

breakdown.label: Link back to safety
breakdown.text: so people do not walk onto the thin ice
breakdown.note: This explains why noticing it matters.

title: A character lies
text:
“Did you eat the last biscuit?” asked Dad.
“No,” said Sam, wiping crumbs from his jumper. He pushed the empty packet under the cushion with his foot.
question: Why does Sam push the empty packet under the cushion?
weakAnswer: He pushes the packet under the cushion.
weakExplanation: That repeats the action, but not the reason.
exceptionalAnswer: Sam pushes the empty packet under the cushion because he is trying to hide the evidence that he ate the last biscuit. The crumbs on his jumper suggest he is lying.

breakdown.label: Reason
breakdown.text: because he is trying to hide the evidence
breakdown.note: This answers why he hides the packet.

breakdown.label: Evidence
breakdown.text: The crumbs on his jumper
breakdown.note: This proves he probably ate the biscuit.

breakdown.label: Explanation
breakdown.text: suggest he is lying
breakdown.note: This explains what the evidence means.

title: A setting description
text: The library was silent except for the ticking clock. Rows of books towered over the tables, and a sign near the door read, Please whisper. Nina lowered her voice before asking for help.
question: Why did Nina lower her voice?
weakExplanation: That says what she did, but not why.
exceptionalAnswer: Nina lowered her voice because she was in a quiet library where people were expected to whisper. The sign says “Please whisper”, so she changed her voice to follow the rule and avoid disturbing others.

breakdown.label: Reason
breakdown.text: because she was in a quiet library
breakdown.note: This explains the situation.

breakdown.label: Evidence
breakdown.text: The sign says ‘Please whisper’
breakdown.note: This uses proof from the text.

breakdown.label: Effect/purpose
breakdown.text: to follow the rule and avoid disturbing others
breakdown.note: This explains why lowering her voice was sensible.

title: A question as a title
text:
Could You Survive on Mars?

Mars is cold, dusty and dry. There is no air for humans to breathe, and temperatures can drop far below freezing. Scientists are trying to discover how people might one day live there.
question: Why is the title written as a question?
weakExplanation: That is true, but it does not explain why the writer chose a question.
exceptionalAnswer: The title is written as a question because it makes the reader curious and encourages them to think about the answer. It also introduces the main topic of the text, which is whether humans could live on Mars.

breakdown.label: Reason/purpose
breakdown.text: because it makes the reader curious
breakdown.note: This explains why the writer used a question.

breakdown.label: Effect on reader
breakdown.text: encourages them to think about the answer
breakdown.note: This explains how the question affects the reader.

breakdown.label: Link back to the text
breakdown.text: It also introduces the main topic... whether humans could live on Mars
breakdown.note: This connects the title to the paragraph.
